mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.5 million signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.5M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3.5% signals a strengthening economy that increases corporate profitability and reduces equity risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 3.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial jobless claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS > 250K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the real short-term interest rate above 2.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL > 2.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 3.5% signals a strengthening economy that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS / CPIAUCSL > 3.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
